description: BudgetChecker.preTurnCheck() is never called before the turn begins. Budget enforcement is completely absent from the turn startup path.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts has no import of BudgetChecker and no call to preTurnCheck. The controller emits hardcoded placeholder events without any budget or rate-limit gate.
suggested_action: Inject BudgetChecker and RateLimiter into AgentTurnController. Call preTurnCheck() after session verification and before emitting turn.started. On 'refused' tier, emit turn.ended with reason 'budget_exceeded' and return 429.

description: None of the Plan 05 §8 OTel metric instruments exist: agent_cost_usd_total, agent_tier_shift_total, agent_provider_fallback_total, agent_budget_remaining_usd, agent_llm_call_attempt_duration_ms, agent_rate_limit_rejection_total, agent_approval_inbox_throttle_total, agent_budget_ceiling_hit_total, agent_degradation_step_total.
evidence: gateway-metrics.ts contains metrics for Plans 01, 02, 12, 14 only. observability-metrics.ts contains Plan 07 meta-metrics only. Grep of the entire agents module finds zero Counter/Histogram instrument definitions for any Plan 05 metric name.
suggested_action: Create apps/api/src/modules/agents/infrastructure/metrics/cost-metrics.ts defining all Plan 05 §8 instruments. Wire increments into CostRecorder, BudgetChecker, RateLimiter, and GracefulDegradationLadder at each decision point.

description: cost event insert and tenant budget decrement are two separate awaited DB calls, not wrapped in a single transaction. A crash between them leaves the budget un-decremented while the cost event exists.
evidence: cost-recorder.ts Step 2 inserts cost event, Step 3 decrements tenant budget, Step 4 upserts user budget — each is a standalone await with no surrounding transaction block. Plan 05 §9 states: 'Cost event insert + budget decrement are within one transaction to prevent partial recording under crash.'
suggested_action: Wrap Steps 2–4 in a Drizzle transaction: db.transaction(async (tx) => { await insertCostEvent(tx); await decrementTenantBudget(tx); await upsertUserBudget(tx); }). Ensure the DB_TOKEN pool client supports nested transaction semantics.

description: MetricLabelGuard.assertNoBlockedLabels() is defined with DEFAULT_BLOCKED_LABELS but is never called at any metric emit site.
evidence: grep across the agents module finds MetricLabelGuard imported only in its own spec file. All metric.add() and histogram.record() calls in gateway-metrics.ts and observability-metrics.ts pass label dicts directly without guard invocation.
suggested_action: Call assertNoBlockedLabels(labels) inside each metric helper function in gateway-metrics.ts and cost-metrics.ts before the OTel record/add call. Add a lint rule or test that verifies every emit helper invokes the guard.

description: GracefulDegradationLadder is implemented but not wired into the turn execution pipeline. Canary-driven steps 4–6 (tier_shift × 2, canary_collapse_refuse) and budget-driven step 7 (budget_refuse) are never triggered during actual turns.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts has no import of GracefulDegradationLadder. quality-canary-subscription.spec.ts tests the ladder in isolation but no integration path connects canary events through the subscription to the active turn's systemAbortController.
suggested_action: Inject GracefulDegradationLadder into the turn execution service. After preTurnCheck determines tier, call ladder.currentStep() to check for canary-forced degradation. Wire QualityCanarySubscription to call systemAbortController.abort() on active turns when step >= 5.

description: Four property-based tests required by Plan 05 §11 are absent: retry-cost invariant (cost(retry_n) >= cost(retry_0)), retry-after fidelity, fallback stickiness, over-billing guard.
evidence: No fast-check or similar property test files found in the agents application/services directory targeting CostRecorder or GracefulDegradationLadder retry behavior.
suggested_action: Add cost-recorder.property.spec.ts using fast-check. Property 1: forall(usage, cost(attempt_1) + cost(attempt_2) >= cost(attempt_1)). Property 2: retry-after header parsed value equals rate-limit window. Property 3: once fallback chosen, next call uses fallback provider. Property 4: total billed never exceeds sum of per-attempt costs.

description: Four integration tests required by Plan 05 §11 are absent: full turn cost split (cache-read vs cache-write tokens), adapter-drop audit trail, mid-turn abort cost partial record, tier-shift integration.
evidence: No integration test files found targeting the cost accounting pipeline end-to-end against a real DB (agentCostEvents + agentTenantBudget writes verified together).
suggested_action: Add cost-pipeline.integration.spec.ts with real Drizzle + pg test DB. Test: (1) complete turn → cost event row inserted + budget decremented atomically; (2) adapter drops usage → adapter_drop audit event recorded; (3) mid-turn abort → partial cost recorded up to abort point; (4) preTurnCheck returns 'nano' tier → nano model used in downstream call.

description: None of the Plan 06 §8 OTel metric instruments exist: agent_turn_total, agent_abort_total, agent_ordering_violation_total, agent_identity_key_write_attempted_total, agent_sse_backpressure_total, agent_turn_force_stopped_total, agent_draft_persist_failure_total, agent_progress_event_total.
evidence: gateway-metrics.ts has no Plan 06 instruments. observability-metrics.ts has Plan 07 meta-metrics only. Grep of the agents module finds zero counter/histogram definitions for any Plan 06 metric name.
suggested_action: Create apps/api/src/modules/agents/infrastructure/metrics/streaming-metrics.ts with all Plan 06 §8 instruments. Wire agent_turn_total increment in ActiveTurnRegistry.register(); agent_abort_total in cancel path; agent_ordering_violation_total in stream-gateway nextState() on invalid transition; agent_turn_force_stopped_total in AgentCancelController force-stop path.

description: The heartbeat setInterval updates lastHeartbeatAt only. It does NOT check the abort_pending column. Cross-pod cancel writes abort_pending=true via CrossPodCancelService but the owning pod never detects it — eventual cross-pod cancel is a dead letter.
evidence: active-turn-registry.ts heartbeat callback: only runs UPDATE SET last_heartbeat_at. cross-pod-cancel.ts writes abortPending=true on forward failure. Plan 06 R-06.40 explicitly requires: 'owning pod detects abort_pending=true on next heartbeat tick and calls userCancelController.abort()'.
suggested_action: In the heartbeat setInterval callback, after updating lastHeartbeatAt, SELECT abort_pending FROM agent_active_turns WHERE trace_id = traceId. If true, call entry.userCancelController.abort() and log the cross-pod cancel detection.

description: draft.proposed persist-then-emit atomicity (R-06.14a) is not implemented. The turn controller emits placeholder events without any draft persistence step.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts emits answer.token and answer.complete directly with no call to a draft repository. Plan 06 R-06.14a requires: 'draft row inserted/updated in DB BEFORE the draft.proposed SSE event is written to the wire; if the DB write fails, the SSE event is suppressed.'
suggested_action: In the actual agent execution path (not the current stub), call agentDraftRepository.upsert() before writeSseEvent('draft.proposed'). If upsert throws, catch and emit turn.ended with reason 'internal_error'; increment agent_draft_persist_failure_total.

description: Fallback visibility event (answer.shape_declared with format='fallback_markdown') is not triggered on provider outage path. The current stub always emits format='markdown'.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts line 114–119: hardcoded emit of answer.shape_declared with format='markdown'. No provider outage detection or fallback format selection exists.
suggested_action: In the actual provider execution path, catch provider outage errors, select the fallback provider, and emit answer.shape_declared with format='fallback_markdown' before streaming the fallback response.

description: stream-gateway.ts close() directly writes turn.ended without calling nextState('turn.ended'), bypassing state machine transition validation. turn.ended can be emitted from states not in TURN_ENDED_ALLOWED set.
evidence: close() checks TERMINAL_STATES.has(state) then directly calls writeSseEvent(JSON.stringify({type:'turn.ended',...})) without going through nextState(). Any intermediate state (shape-declared, tokens-streaming) can produce a turn.ended, violating the SSE state machine contract.
suggested_action: Refactor close() to call this.nextState('turn.ended') in a try/catch. If the transition is invalid, force-emit turn.ended anyway but also increment agent_ordering_violation_total counter. This preserves wire correctness while recording the violation.

description: When activeTurnRegistry.cancel(traceId) is called for a force-stop, the canceller's userId is not propagated to the abort payload. The turn.ended event cannot include cancelled_by in the SSE wire payload.
evidence: agent-cancel-controller.ts line 112: this.activeTurnRegistry.cancel(traceId) — no cancelledBy argument. ActiveTurnRegistry.cancel() calls userCancelController.abort() with no reason/payload carrying the actor identity.
suggested_action: Add optional cancelledBy?: string param to ActiveTurnRegistry.cancel(). Pass it to userCancelController.abort({ cancelledBy }) using AbortController reason. In stream-gateway.close(), read signal.reason?.cancelledBy and include it in turn.ended payload.

description: Three integration tests required by Plan 06 §11 are absent: persist-then-emit atomicity (R-06.14a DB write failure suppresses SSE), fallback visibility end-to-end, provider outage complete path.
evidence: No integration test files found for stream-gateway or agent-turn-controller exercising real SSE event sequences against provider outage or DB failure scenarios.
suggested_action: Add streaming-pipeline.integration.spec.ts. Test 1: mock draft DB write to throw → verify no draft.proposed on SSE wire. Test 2: mock primary provider to throw 503 → verify answer.shape_declared format='fallback_markdown' emitted. Test 3: cancel during streaming → verify turn.ended reason='cancelled' and no events after it.

description: kernel audit_event table has no flow_id or intent_slug columns. Plan 07 §3 requires these as first-class indexed columns for trace-audit join and per-intent dashboard.
evidence: audit-event.schema.ts columns: id, tenantId, actorId, eventType, module, subjectId, payload, createdAt. No flow_id, no intent_slug. Plan 07 §3: 'kernel_audit_event must expose flow_id UUID and intent_slug varchar columns; a follow-up kernel migration is required before this plan ships.'
suggested_action: Add flowId uuid and intentSlug varchar(120) columns to kernel audit_event schema. Squash into 0000_initial.sql per the project migration rule. Update KernelAuditFacade.recordEvent() signature to accept and persist these fields. Update all callers in agents module to pass flow_id from ObservabilityContext.

description: ObservabilityContextFactory.create() is never called from any production turn path. No TURN root span is ever created. Every turn produces zero OTel spans, making R-07.43 (flow_id/intent_slug on every span, hard fail if missing) vacuously pass but fundamentally broken.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts: no import of ObservabilityContextFactory, FlowIdPropagation, or any OTel span creation. Grep of agents module: ObservabilityContextFactory referenced only in extensibility-invariant-audit.ts as a comment. FlowIdPropagation.mint() not called anywhere in production paths.
suggested_action: In the turn execution service, after session verification: (1) call FlowIdPropagation.mint() to get flowId; (2) call ObservabilityContextFactory.create(requestContext, flowId, intentSlug, samplingConfig) to get ObservabilityContext with root span; (3) pass context through the execution pipeline; (4) close root span in finally block.

description: The daily cross-tenant leak canary job always records 'clean' without performing any actual scan. This provides false security assurance — real cross-tenant trace leaks would go undetected.
evidence: leak-canary.scheduler.ts run(): always calls recordLeakCanary('clean') without any scan logic. Comment: 'MVP stub — trace backend not yet deployed.' Plan 07 §6 R-07.38a marks this as MVP-active, not deferred.
suggested_action: Either: (a) implement the actual scan against whatever trace storage is available (even a simple DB query for cross-tenant audit event mismatches), or (b) formally defer R-07.38a and update the plan's MVP/deferred classification to prevent false confidence. Do not leave always-clean stub silently active.

description: DEFAULT_COMPOSITION_SENSITIVE_TOOLS is an empty Set. The composition monitor will never emit agent.composition_pattern_observed because sensitiveInvocations is always empty regardless of actual tool calls.
evidence: composition-monitor.worker.ts: DEFAULT_COMPOSITION_SENSITIVE_TOOLS = new Set(). Comment: 'Empty at MVP — populated when Plan 01 ships tool metadata.' The monitor job runs but produces no signal. R-07.46 is structurally present but functionally unmet.
suggested_action: Either populate DEFAULT_COMPOSITION_SENSITIVE_TOOLS with at least a sentinel tool name and document the expected expansion when Plan 01 ships, or add a mandatory non-empty check that throws at startup if the set is empty (forcing the gap to be visible). Add an integration test that seeds a tool invocation record and asserts the pattern event fires.

description: OtelSpan.recordUsage() uses console.warn when called on a non-leaf span instead of incrementing the agent_usage_recorded_on_non_leaf_total counter defined in observability-metrics.ts.
evidence: span.ts recordUsage(): if (this._hasChildren) { console.warn('recordUsage called on non-leaf span...'); return; }. The counter instrument exists in observability-metrics.ts but is never referenced or incremented from span.ts.
suggested_action: Import the agent_usage_recorded_on_non_leaf_total counter into span.ts (or inject via constructor). Replace console.warn with counter.add(1, { span_type: this._type, tenant_id: this._tenantId }). Keep the early return to suppress double-counting.

description: TurnSamplingDecisionRecorder is registered in the agents module but never called from the turn lifecycle. Sampling decisions are never persisted to agent_turn_sampling_decisions.
evidence: agent-turn-controller.ts: no import of TurnSamplingDecisionRecorder. The table agentTurnSamplingDecisions exists in schema but accumulates no rows. SamplingConfig is never consulted during turn startup.
suggested_action: In the turn execution flow, after creating ObservabilityContext (which holds the sampling decision), call TurnSamplingDecisionRecorder.record(traceId, flowId, samplingDecision) before emitting turn.started. This creates the audit trail required by R-07.6.

description: request-context-discipline.ts records a kernel audit event and logs on identity key write attempts but does not increment the agent_identity_key_write_attempted_total OTel counter.
evidence: request-context-discipline.ts violation handler: console.error + kernelAuditFacade.recordEvent(). The counter instrument agent_identity_key_write_attempted_total is defined in observability-metrics.ts but never referenced from this service.
suggested_action: Inject or import the agent_identity_key_write_attempted_total counter into RequestContextDiscipline. Increment it on every violation, with labels { environment: prod|dev, key_attempted }. This enables the Grafana cardinality dashboard required by Plan 07 §8.

description: trace_retention_days and audit_retention_days columns not found in admin_tenant_config schema. Plan 07 §3 requires these for per-tenant trace/audit retention configuration.
evidence: Grep of admin module schema files finds no trace_retention_days or audit_retention_days column definitions. These are referenced in Plan 07 §3 as required columns on the tenant config table.
suggested_action: Add traceRetentionDays integer (default 90) and auditRetentionDays integer (default 365) columns to admin tenant config schema. Squash into 0000_initial.sql. Expose via AdminTenantConfigQueryFacade for the observability infrastructure to consume.

description: No test verifies that unclassified intent_slug usage stays at or below 2% of total spans (R-07.48). The ceiling is stated in the plan but not enforced by any automated check.
evidence: No spec file found testing intent_slug classification rates or asserting the 2% unclassified ceiling across a sample of turns.
suggested_action: Add an integration test that runs N synthetic turns with intentionally unclassified slugs and asserts the ratio of 'unclassified' intent_slug entries in agent_turn_sampling_decisions stays below 0.02. Wire this as a CI gate.

description: GracefulDegradationLadder steps 2/3 (provider_retry/provider_fallback) and steps 4/5/6 (canary tier_shift × 2, canary_collapse_refuse) share the same enum shape. The discriminator between tier_shift and provider_fallback is not enforced at type level — a caller could conflate them.
evidence: ladder.ts LadderStep enum: steps are numeric 1–7 with string tags. No TypeScript branded type or discriminated union prevents passing a tier_shift step index where a provider_fallback step is expected in metric label construction.
suggested_action: Add a discriminated union type: type ProviderStep = { kind: 'provider'; step: 1|2|3 } | { kind: 'canary'; step: 4|5|6 } | { kind: 'budget'; step: 7 }. Use this in metric label construction to make conflation a compile error. Update Plan 05 §8 metric labels to include kind dimension.
